text: The surgeon establishes pneumoperitoneum using a Veress needle and places laparoscopic ports. A liver retractor is deployed, and the duodenum is circumferentially mobilized using blunt dissection with laparoscopic instruments and a ligature energy device. The dissection reveals the posterior wall and tumor boundaries, avoiding the portal triad structures. The mass is confirmed not to extend into surrounding structures.

The surgeon uses a hook cautery to create an enterotomy in the proximal duodenum. A ligature energy device is employed to circumferentially resect the mass, ensuring hemostasis and maintaining a one-centimeter margin around it. The excised tumor is placed in an endocatch bag and set aside.

The surgeon performs a transverse repair of the duodenum using 2-0 Ethibond sutures in a simple interrupted fashion, creating a single-layer closure. An upper endoscopy is used to evaluate the repair, with air insufflation of the stomach and duodenum and saline filling of the peritoneum to check for leaks, which are absent. The operation concludes with the placement of a number ten Jackson Pratt drain at the repair site.
